_note: Guided tour as a slide deck. stages[] -> {name,badge,stops[]}. Each stop is one vertical slide: title, image (ONE only; site photos /images/... , map aerials + CABN photos media/...), caption (optional, under image), lead (1-2 sentences), points[] (bullets), layers[] (slugs shown), fit ('all'=whole property), cta (optional). 'Operating now' badge renders green. intro = the start lightbox.

intro.title: Wells Gray — the Master Plan
intro.body:
393 acres of private land beside Wells Gray Provincial Park, near Clearwater, BC.

This guided tour walks through what's already built and operating, then the plan to grow a village on the land — cabins and a maker space, festival grounds, an RV & tiny-home expansion, a boutique hotel, and a car-free wellness village with a farm and lakeside spa.

13 short stops. Move with the arrows; close anytime to explore the map yourself.

stops.title: The Land
stops.lead: 393 acres at the gateway to Wells Gray Provincial Park, near Clearwater, BC.
points: Private land — lake, creek, forest and open meadow
Phase 1 is built and operating today
A walk through what's here, and the village planned on top of it

stops.title: Built & Operating
stops.lead: Five seasons in — the working prototype the village builds on.
points: Full-service & power/water RV sites, plus rental cabins
120-person event gazebo with a commercial kitchen
Geodesic glamping dome, cedar sauna & cold plunge
Private swimming lake, shower houses & disc-golf course

stops.title: Maker Space
stops.lead: A community workshop — and the engine of the whole build.
points: 3D printers, CNC, laser cutter, woodworking
Fabricates and finishes the village's cabins & tiny homes
Wired off the existing electrical trunk — a short feed, no new service

stops.title: The Cabins
stops.caption: Cabin model: CABN — MOR.II (cabn.co)
stops.lead: Small net-zero prefab cabins woven into the forest.
points: Plug into existing power, water, septic & Starlink — short spurs only
Built by CABN (cabn.co): BC-interior, net-zero, solar-ready
Range from the HUTT studio (~$39.5k) to the MOR.II (540 sqft)
CABN ships the timber shell; the maker space finishes the interiors

stops.title: Festival Grounds
stops.lead: Light-touch by design — no permanent build.
points: Clear the meadow and cut/clear the trail network
Add a new shower house and grouped portapotties
Runs a 500-to-5,000-person, leave-no-trace festival
Cleared timber is milled on site for later-phase builds

stops.title: Maker Village
stops.lead: Prefab cabins for the people who build the village.
points: Housing paired with shared maker infrastructure
Built on-site to a repeatable, cloneable template
Full power, water, septic & Wi-Fi run to the cluster

stops.title: RV & Tiny-Home Sites
stops.lead: A tidy expansion of the existing campground.
points: Pull-through RV pads and tiny-home sites
Upgraded 50-amp power, water and septic
A new transformer and septic field serve the cluster

stops.title: Boutique Hotel
stops.lead: A small lodge near the entrance for guests and prospective members.
points: Rooms for visitors who aren't camping
Low, tucked-in building footprint
Ties into existing power, water & septic — the mains run right past

stops.title: Land for Sale
stops.lead: Large acreage lots — the capital that funds the build.
points: A handful of lots offered for sale
Two layouts: uniform ~10-acre, or a 10 / 5 / 2.5-acre mix
Toggle the options in the layers panel

stops.title: Artist Village
stops.lead: A quiet cabin community above the lake — art as infrastructure.
points: Studios, residencies and creative work
Four cabin types: small, medium, hillside, cliffside-hempcrete
Linked by walking paths through the forest

stops.title: Wellness Village & The Commons
stops.lead: The heart of the residential vision.
points: Clusters of cabin "pods" around shared greens
Spread across the old golf course
Every path converges on The Commons — café, maker space, co-working, movement + wellness studios

stops.title: Farm & Lakeside Spa
stops.lead: A regenerative farm threads between the pods; a spa sits on the pond.
points: Contour beds, a mandala garden, food forest, greenhouses & pond
Feeds residents and the wider network
Lakeside Spa: sauna, cold-plunge, soaking deck, treatment cabins

stops.title: The Bigger Picture
stops.lead: Wells Gray is the flagship — and the blueprint for a network.
points: A real, operating prototype
A template for aligned villages elsewhere
